mainmatter.p: Following my meeting with Marcelo, I started to resurrect the old Agda-flat code for tininess from LOPS using the present-day --cohesion option. I managed to define the substitution function and it does pass the termination checker, but it doesn’t compute in Agda (even using --rewriting.)

I discussed with Marcelo the definition of single-variable substitution in the object classifier using the tininess of the interval. I plan to try and define this in Agda-flat; I am curious if I can get it to compute, using rewrite rules for the “amazing” transpose.
We also discussed the plan for the Discrete Mathematics course. My lectures should start somewhere in the set theory section, and then continue through regular languages and automata to the end.

mainmatter.blockquote: Interests: Mathematical models of computation; denotational and operational semantics of programming languages and interactive systems; type systems and program logics for functional and concurrent computation; applications of category theory to computer science.

frontmatter.title: Internal universes in models of homotopy type theory
frontmatter.meta: @inproceedings{lops-2018, author = {Licata, Daniel R. and Orton, Ian and Pitts, Andrew M. and Spitters, Bas}, booktitle = {3rd International Conference on Formal Structures for Computation and Deduction, {FSCD} 2018, July 9-12, 2018, Oxford, {UK}}, year = {2018}, doi = {10.4230/LIPIcs.FSCD.2018.22}, pages = {22:1--22:17}, title = {Internal Universes in Models of Homotopy Type Theory}, }
3rd International Conference on Formal Structures for Computation and Deduction
